kits.story: The adidas home jersey features a chalk white base overlaid with subtle golden textured vertical stripes evoking wind-carved Saharan sand dunes. 'Algeria' is inscribed in Arabic script at the back of the neck, and the jersey uses the national flag as the chest crest instead of the federation emblem — a cultural first. The retro adidas Trefoil returns to the tournament for the first time in 36 years.

A visual tribute to Algeria's diverse geography beyond the desert — rocky terrains, lush oases and Mediterranean coastline. The two-tone green design pairs darker green sleeves and shoulders with a lighter green torso covered in vertical tonal stripes and geometric patterns, while a red ribbed V-neck collar provides a pop of the national flag.

The adidas pre-match shirt features a light beige base with a swirling graphic pattern in tones of sand and deep green, with solid green sleeves. The abstract pattern is distinct from the match-day shirts, using AEROREADY moisture-wicking technology.

links.title: Algeria 2026 jersey: adidas away kit

kits.story: The adidas Argentina 2026 home jersey refreshes the iconic albiceleste stripes with a three-tone blue gradient fading through each sky-blue band, with each shade drawn from their triumphant 1978, 1986 and 2022 campaigns. Navy shoulder stripes and sleeve cuffs frame the design, while a gold-accented '1896' detail on the back neck marks the AFA's founding year.

The away jersey is predominantly black with sky-blue accents and an intricate swirling pattern inspired by fileteado porteño, the UNESCO-recognized folk art tradition of Buenos Aires characterized by spiral lines, ornate floral swirls and climbing plants that have adorned the city's carts, buses and buildings since the late 19th century. A bespoke 'Argentina' motif sits in front of the Sol de Mayo sun symbol on the back neck, and the adidas Trefoil returns to the tournament for the first time in 36 years.

Features a dynamic sunburst pattern radiating outward from the upper chest in sky blue, white and navy stripes — a modern interpretation of the Sol de Mayo from Argentina's national flag. Metallic gold adidas logo and AFA crest sit centrally.

links.title: Rating adidas' 2026 away shirts: Argentina, Spain, Mexico & more

kits.story: Nike's Socceroos home shirt pays tribute to the iconic 2006 gold jersey that captured the nation's heart during Australia's run to the round of 16 in Germany. The rich gold base is paired with mystic green accents on the collar and sleeve cuffs, channeling the Total 90 era that defined a generation of Australian football fans.

Inspired by Australian sunrises, the away jersey features a striking coral-pink upper chest that fades into deep teal green toward the hem — the same colors seen on an early morning coastal walk. A lenticular federation crest shifts dimensionally with movement, connecting the symbolic weight of the badge to the act of wearing it. Made from 100% textile waste.

kits.story: Austria's signature red body features a historic first — black sleeves on a home jersey, a bold departure from the traditional all-red look. Red, white and black cuffs on the sleeves reference the Austrian national flag, and the back collar carries the inscription 'Immer wieder Osterreich' ('Austria, again and again').

The away kit features a white and light mint-green base with a marble-texture pattern designed to evoke the surfaces of classic Viennese coffeehouse tables. Curved golden geometric lines inspired by Art Nouveau architecture arch across the front, with turquoise and magenta accents throughout — blending Austria's coffeehouse culture with modern streetwear.
A navy and red pre-match jersey layered with a decorative Art Deco finial pattern over the entire design, featuring dryCELL moisture-wicking technology and 100% recycled material.

Puma unveils Austria 2026 away kit

kits.story: adidas' Belgium home shirt is deep red with gold and black detailing, covered in an all-over pattern that merges tulip shapes, tridents and flames into a fiery devil motif — symbolizing the shared spirit of the men's Red Devils and women's Red Flames. The shirt also carries a creative tribute to Brussels icon Manneken Pis.

The adidas Originals away shirt is a robin's-egg blue and pink design based on the work of Belgian surrealist René Magritte, its repeated ball shapes echoing the apples, moons and suns of his paintings, with fine horizontal lines referencing the markings of a pitch. Script at the collar reads 'Ceci n'est pas un maillot' ('This is not a jersey'), an homage to The Treachery of Images — the fourth time Belgium's away kit has celebrated national culture, after cycling (2016), Tomorrowland (2022) and Tintin (2024).

Belgium's anthem jacket mirrors the home shirt: a red base with bold yellow adidas Three Stripes over the shoulders extending down the sleeves, directly echoing the stripe application on the home kit.

links.title: adidas designed Belgium's jerseys for elite athletes — and clever art buffs

kits.story: Kelme's Bosnia and Herzegovina home kit features a deep navy blue base with thin vertical pinstripes alternating between white and yellow across the front. The design draws from the movement of Bosnia's rivers, symbolizing the nation's journey flowing with pride from its roots to the international stage. The kit carries the slogan 'Dream Big Dream BiH' reflecting the nation's pride in reaching the tournament.

The away kit features a predominantly white base with solid blue raglan-style sleeves and a matching blue V-neck collar. Thin vertical pinstripes in blue and yellow run down the front, keeping the national identity alive while providing contrast. Designed by Sarajevo-based designer Haris Stambolic.

kits.story: Nike's Brazil 2026 home shirt keeps the traditional canary-yellow base with green trim, blue shorts and white socks, and its overall look nods to the shirt worn by the 1970 title-winning side. A subtle all-over graphic inspired by a distorted, geometric rendering of the Brazilian flag runs through the fabric, with brighter green accents on the shoulders and sides and Nike's new rounded notch collar.

The away kit is Jordan Brand's first-ever national-team football kit, the product of a landmark partnership between the brand and the Brazilian football confederation. Its royal-blue base carries an updated version of Jordan's late-1980s 'elephant print' in a colour scheme inspired by the poison dart frog, the small, vividly coloured and highly toxic rainforest amphibian, finished with yellow Jumpman branding and aqua side panels.

Jordan Brand's away pre-match shirt uses a blue-and-black mottled graphic that directly mimics the skin of the blue poison dart frog, extending the away kit's theme, with the yellow Jumpman and CBF crest placed centrally on the chest.

kits.story: For the Blue Sharks' first-ever tournament appearance, Capelli Sport designed a deep blue home jersey featuring a geometric triangular pattern representing flight paths connecting the ten volcanic islands of the Cape Verdean archipelago. Red trim on the collar and cuffs echoes the national flag, while the 3D silicone FCVF crest sits over the heart.

The away kit is white with a subtle tonal grey version of the same flight-path geometric pattern, paired with blue and red detailing. It represents the clarity and ambition of a small island nation stepping onto the global stage for the first time.

The pre-match shirt features a blue base covered in an abstract white shark jaw pattern, symbolizing the team's predatory mindset — composed, patient and always hunting — reflecting the determination of a small, fierce team with nothing to lose and everything to gain.

kits.story: Nike's red home shirt centers a split-tone maple leaf pointing north, the anchor of the 'Full Tilt' collection built around Canada Soccer's 'True North' identity. The collar closure carries a loon badge reading 'From coast to coast to coast' in English and French, referencing the Pacific, Arctic and Atlantic coasts, and the kit was led by Calgary-born Nike designer Holly Gallacher.

The black away shirt carries a scattered white cracked-ice graphic inspired by frozen landscapes and 'black ice,' with a frozen maple leaf etched like a skate blade as a nod to Canada's winter-sports heritage. University Red accents the Nike Swoosh and Canada Soccer crest against the black base.

Nike's official Canada anthem jacket layers a double-layered black mesh outer over a red inner lining, with the Swoosh and Canada Soccer crest applied in red and a taped chevron line across the chest.

kits.story: The adidas Colombia 2026 home jersey features a vibrant yellow foundation with blue and red flag-inspired accents, overlaid with a delicate butterfly pattern paying tribute to Gabriel García Márquez's magical realism — the yellow butterflies from One Hundred Years of Solitude symbolize transformation, hope and Colombia's unique blend of fantasy and reality.

The away jersey celebrates Colombia's biodiversity by blending the colors of its two coastlines — the darker blues of the Pacific Ocean with the lighter tones of the Caribbean Sea — brought to life through staggered vertical lines that create a shimmering, fish-scale-like effect. Bold yellow trim across the three-stripes, cuffs and logos evoke pride and connection to the land.

A culturally rich tribute to García Márquez's literary heritage, featuring a warm gradient base in yellow, orange and soft pink covered in a dynamic all-over graphic of fluttering butterflies in varying shades of blue and yellow — referencing the famous swarms of yellow butterflies from One Hundred Years of Solitude.

kits.story: Umbro's Congo DR home jersey celebrates the Leopards' identity with a vibrant light blue base featuring a tone-on-tone leopard-print texture and wavy graphic pattern across the lower half. Bold red trim on the round collar and sleeve cuffs mirrors the Congolese flag, with the FECOFA crest topped by two stars recognizing the nation's historic 1968 and 1974 African championship titles.

The away kit features a crisp white base with an eye-catching geometric diamond pattern transitioning from white into subtle blue tones across the lower section. Bright blue accent panels run along the shoulders and sides, creating a clean modern look.

kits.story: Nike's Croatia home jersey returns to a classic approach with bold red-and-white checkers running vertically down the center, directly inspired by the kit worn in Croatia's 1990 match against the United States — the nation's inaugural match as an independent country. Solid red sleeves and blue Hyper Royal accents frame the design, with the word 'Obitelj' (Family) printed inside the collar.

The away jersey transposes the checkered design onto a deep navy base with tonal two-tone checkers creating a sophisticated, subdued presence. Thin red and lighter blue details run along the sides for national flair. This is the last kit produced by Nike for Croatia, ending a 26-year partnership before adidas takes over in August 2026.

kits.story: Designed under adidas's 'Island Identity' theme for Curaçao's historic first tournament appearance, the home shirt features a lucid blue base with impact yellow trim and a graphic pattern of lighter blue, wavy circular lines across the sleeves inspired by the ocean surrounding the Caribbean island.

The away kit celebrates Willemstad's UNESCO World Heritage neighborhoods through vibrant aesthetics — a pastel yellow base with bold pink, turquoise, and orange stripes referencing the iconic painted colonial buildings of Handelskade and Otrobanda. Intricate blue detailing appears throughout the sleeves and cuffs.

kits.story: The Czechia home kit honors the heroes of Euro '96 with proud nods to the iconic double-tailed lion paired with silver detailing. The design celebrates the courage, unity, and pride behind that legendary campaign. Part of Puma's 'Collective Joy and Cultural Texture' theme, featuring RE:FIBRE recycled material construction.

The away jersey features an icy white base with an intricate all-over crystal-inspired graphic print, a nod to Bohemian crystal craftsmanship that has defined Czech artistry for centuries. Gold accents on the ribbed crew-neck collar and side panels add a premium touch.

kits.story: The home shirt features an all-over tonal animal-print texture in vivid Rickie Orange, drawing from traditional Ivorian textiles and symbols to create movement, festivity, and warmth. Sport Green trims line the collar and side panels, referencing the tricolor of the Ivorian flag. Part of Puma's 'Collective Joy and Cultural Texture' theme, celebrating the vibrancy of Ivorian life. Made with at least 95% recycled material.

Built on a clean white foundation, the away shirt features an intricate all-over botanical print of palm trees, baobab flowers, and radiating sun-like floral motifs rendered in soft orange and mint green. Inspired by the country's vibrant fashion culture and natural landscapes, the design evokes sunlight filtering through foliage.

kits.story: Marathon Sports' Ecuador home kit is predominantly yellow with a sophisticated horizontal texture and high-relief crest. A navy collar with red detailing and navy sleeve cuffs with red accents represent the Ecuadorian flag. All three Ecuador kits carry the motto 'Sonar, Trascender y Hacer Historia' (Dream, Transcend and Make History) on the upper back.

The away jersey features a deep navy blue base with a subtle tonal pattern and a structured polo-style collar. Copper-gold detailing on the Marathon logo and numbering adds a premium touch. Graphic side panels and advanced Hidrotec moisture-wicking technology complete the design.

kits.story: Puma's Egypt 2026 home jersey channels the theme 'Ancient Power, Modern Ambition' with a rich red base overlaid with angular geometric patterns referencing both ancient hieroglyphics and the energy of modern Cairo. A symmetrical arrangement of pyramid-like shapes radiates from a central ankh symbol — the ancient Egyptian icon of life — while black and gold accents reinforce prestige.

The away kit uses a soft white base layered with tonal abstract patterns evoking desert sands, shifting light and timeless landscapes rendered in motion. Subtle pyramid-inspired geometric shapes and sphinx illustrations create intricate textures reminiscent of ancient hieroglyphics, with a dark green collar trim grounding the design.

kits.story: Nike's England home shirt is classic white with flags, lions and stars engineered directly into the jacquard-like fabric texture, plus thin navy piping along the shoulders and red side inserts edged in navy. A single gold star for 1966 sits above the crest, 'HAPPY AND GLORIOUS' is printed inside the collar, and the shirt is woven from Nike's Aero-FIT cooling fabric made from 100 per cent textile waste.

The away kit pairs a 'Speed Red' base with Obsidian navy and white accents, England's first red away shirt since 2022, worn with navy shorts. The Three Lions crest and Swoosh are centred on the chest beneath the gold star, echoing celebrated England away shirts of the past, and it carries the same tonal lions-and-flags iconography as the home in Nike's Aero-FIT fabric.

The pre-match shirt headlines Nike's Palace Skateboards × England capsule for the 2026 tournament, featuring an all-over stained-glass St George graphic in Palace's signature aesthetic with the England crest and a single star on the chest; it released 12 June 2026 in Palace stores ahead of a wider Nike launch.

Nike 2026 England home shirt review

kits.story: Nike's navy home shirt pairs 'Game Royal' blue with metallic copper — a nod to the oxidized copper of the Statue of Liberty, France's gift to co-host USA — over an allover woven FFF-motif pattern. A modernized white polo collar with a hidden red detail in the closure completes the tricolor reference.

Named 'Liberté,' the away shirt is the first green jersey in the French national team's history: its pale Igloo green mirrors the Statue of Liberty's weathered patina, while copper-orange 'Monarch' accents recall the statue's original metal. Tricolor sleeve trims tie the concept back to the flag.

Nike and French fashion house Jacquemus co-created France's anthem jacket — a white hooded full-zip with tonal Swoosh, FFF cockerel crest and Jacquemus wordmark, plus tricolor taping down both sleeves. Les Bleus first wore it during the anthems in June 2026.

kits.story: The last-ever adidas Germany home shirt features a clean white base with a striking tricolour diamond and chevron pattern in black, red and gold, echoing the iconic graphics worn by the 1990 and 2014 tournament winners. Four stars above the crest commemorate Germany's victories in 1954, 1974, 1990 and 2014. The kit marks the end of a partnership stretching back to 1954 before Nike takes over in 2027.

Built on Germany's traditional blue training-top heritage, the away kit reimagines diagonal chevrons as an all-over pattern in navy blue with aqua accents. The design references the classic blue quarter-zips worn in 1954, the blue-and-white training tops of the 1960s through 1980s, and the bold coloured training wear of the 1990s. The classic adidas Trefoil returns for this farewell collection.

links.title: Germany Football 2026 kits: home, away, release dates & prices

kits.story: Ghana's home kit features a predominantly white base with a bold, multicolored graphic of intersecting lines in red, yellow and green inspired by Kente, the iconic woven cloth of Ghanaian heritage. The cobweb-like pattern across the torso is a direct nod to Kwaku Ananse, the legendary spider figure from West African folktales who uses wit and storytelling to overcome larger foes. Designed under the creative direction of renowned Ghanaian visual artist Prince Gyasi.

The away jersey introduces a vivid 'Sunny Yellow' base with Archive Gold detailing, drawing inspiration from the vibrant energy of Accra's famous Makola Market. The fabric is covered in an all-over tonal graphic of layered geometric shapes and repeating Adinkra symbols inspired by Kente cloth — the hand-woven textile that has represented Ghanaian royalty, spirituality and identity for centuries.
A dark crimson and red pre-match jersey featuring dryCELL moisture-wicking technology, designed to complement the culturally inspired match-day kits with a sleek warm-up look.

links.title: Ghana Football 2026 kits: home, away & release dates

kits.story: Made by Colombian sportswear brand Saeta, Haiti's home jersey features a deep blue base with vibrant red detailing around the crew-neck collar and sleeve cuffs. The original design featured tonal graphics inspired by the Battle of Vertières, the final act of the Haitian Revolution in 1803, though elements were modified during the approval process.

The away kit features a clean white base with red polo collar and cuff trim, sharing the same heritage design language and dual-fabric mountain-pattern construction as the home. Grey-gradient sleeves add a sophisticated modern touch. This marks Haiti's historic return to the tournament for the first time since 1974.

Football 2026 kit ranking: all 105 jerseys for all 48 teams

kits.story: Produced by domestic brand Majid, Team Melli's home kit features the traditional white base with the return of the prominent Asiatic Cheetah — Iran's endangered national animal — as a large grey watermark on the lower right torso. Stripes in the national flag colors of green, white, and red appear on the collar and cuffs.

The away kit shares the same design template as the home but in red, with a slightly darker Asiatic Cheetah pattern creating a tonal effect. The red base represents the courage element of the Iranian flag's tricolor symbolism.

links.title: Football 2026 kit ranking: all 105 jerseys for all 48 teams

kits.story: Produced by German brand Jako, the Iraq home kit features a white base with an all-over design of circular dot clusters and interlocking diamond shapes inspired by ancient mosaics from Uruk, one of the earliest cities in Mesopotamian civilization. The geometric patterns create visual depth while maintaining a clean, modern silhouette.

The away jersey uses a deep green base — a color historically associated with Iraqi football — with the same Mesopotamian-inspired geometric patterning in white. Both kits feature Jako's high-quality sublimation printing to render the traditional patterns directly into the fabric.

links.title: Iraq Football 2026 jersey: new Jako home & away kits released
Football 2026 kit ranking: all 105 jerseys for all 48 teams

kits.story: Under the theme 'Beyond the Horizon,' the home jersey uses Japan's classic blue enhanced with ash blue linear details reflecting the famed haze where sky and sea meet along the Japanese coastline. The Japanese flag sits across the back of the neck as a nod to national pride. Built with Climacool+ technology for on-pitch cooling.

Widely praised as one of the best jerseys of the tournament, the away kit features 12 vertical stripes on an off-white base — 11 fading lines representing each player on the pitch, and a bold 12th central stripe in the red of the Japanese flag symbolizing the fans as the heart of the team. The retro adidas Trefoil logo returns for this collection.

kits.story: Designed by Spanish manufacturer Kelme in collaboration with Jordanian designers, the home jersey features a clean white base with a striking red diamond-dot gradient pattern across the shoulders and upper sleeves inspired by the traditional kufiya/shemagh — one of Jordan's most prominent emblems of national identity.

The away kit is a direct color inversion of the home, using a predominantly red jersey with white kufiya-inspired diamond detailing across the shoulders and sleeves. A small eagle graphic on the upper back references Jordan's coat of arms.

links.title: Jordan Football 2026 jersey: Kelme home, away & third kits released
Football 2026 kit ranking: all 105 jerseys for all 48 teams

kits.story: Centred around Nike's 'Tiger's Ambush' narrative, the home kit arrives in Global Red with an all-over sublimated tiger stripe pattern layered in darker red tones, creating a textured, almost camouflage-like effect with a sense of movement. Black side panels run from the underarm to the hem, edged with narrow white trim. The design blends traditional Korean folklore with modern, aggressive footballing philosophy.

Where the home kit channels tiger stealth, the away blooms with intention — a floral-inspired graphic in Space Purple channels Korea's emotional and cultural dynamism. The botanical motif represents condensed energy and explosive momentum, while the violet colour is a purposeful choice to reinforce surprise and individuality. Built with Nike's Aero-FIT technology for unprecedented cooling.

kits.story: adidas' Mexico home shirt overlays a deep green base with a large Aztec-inspired motif of the Piedra del Sol (Aztec Sun Stone), with use of the motif authorised through work with the National Anthropology Museum in Mexico City, which houses the stone. The design is a homage to Mexico's iconic 1998 tournament shirt, and 'SOMOS MÉXICO' ('We Are Mexico') sits across the back of the neck; white shorts and red socks complete the look.

The white away shirt carries a subtle all-over grey graphic reinterpreting 'Grecas', the stepped geometric patterns found in traditional Mexican art and architecture, with dark green and red detailing on the collar, cuffs and three stripes. Like all adidas 2026 federation away kits, it wears the retro Trefoil logo on the right chest, marking the Trefoil's first appearance at the tournament in 36 years.

The pre-match shirt covers a vibrant green base with a dynamic interlocking geometric graphic built from Aztec-styled 'M' and 'X' letterforms, a pattern inspired by the hand-crafted work of rural Mexican artisans.

kits.story: Puma's deep red home shirt uses green accents echoing the star of the Moroccan flag, with gold detailing and collar-and-cuff patterns drawn from traditional Moroccan embroidery and zellige-style geometric tilework. Puma and the FRMF presented the collection as a tribute to Moroccan craftsmanship, ancestral geometric motifs and Amazigh heritage.

The white away shirt carries a gold-toned textured print inspired directly by traditional Amazigh (Berber) embroidery running down the shirt, finished with red-and-green trim at the collar and cuffs. Red Tifinagh (Amazigh) lettering sits below the back neckline, a nod to the languages spoken across Morocco.
Puma's official Morocco pre-match jersey comes in crimson with team graphics and the heat-sealed FRMF crest, built with dryCELL moisture-wicking fabric in a slim raglan-sleeve cut.

kits.story: Nike engineered a new 'Hyper Crimson' orange specifically for this kit — the most vibrant and saturated shade ever used on a Netherlands jersey. The torso features a geometric lion knit pattern that carries down through the shorts, while a lenticular, iridescent KNVB crest shifts color as the jersey moves. A custom crown graphic inside the collar highlights the federation's royal connection.

Built around the concept of 'microscopic gradients,' the away kit features a white base with a bold horizontal band across the chest that fades through shifting shades of orange. The design reflects Dutch values of precision and experimentation — from Dutch design and architecture to the Total Football philosophy that redefined the global game in the 1970s.

kits.story: The All Whites' home kit features a bold black base with an all-over silver fern graphic — New Zealand's most recognizable national symbol. White accents frame the Puma logo and crest, while 'Aotearoa' appears beneath the back collar, honoring the country's Māori name.

The away jersey features a white base with a swirling graphic inspired by Ngā Hau e Whā — the four winds of Māori tradition. Black accents on the crest, logo, and side panels provide contrast, with 'Aotearoa' again on the back neckline. The design draws from New Zealand's natural landscape and cultural heritage.

links.title: New Zealand Football 2026 kits: home, away, release dates & prices

kits.story: Nike's Norway home shirt is classic red, wrapped in an oversized interpretation of the Norwegian flag: a blue Nordic cross outlined in white spans the chest and torso, with an intricate tonal knotwork pattern embedded in the blue fabric and repeated on the sleeve bands. Names and numbers are printed in a custom typeface Nike created with Norway inspired by Elder Futhark, the oldest form of the Norse runic alphabet.

The away kit is all black, down to a black-on-black federation crest, a stealth look that breaks from Norway's traditional away designs. Viking-inspired patterns appear on the sleeves and socks, and the kit shares the tournament's custom Elder Futhark runic name-and-number set.
Norway's hooded full-zip anthem jacket from Nike's 2026 range uses double-layered Dri-FIT mesh for airflow, with the black outer mesh revealing a teal underlayer and the Norway crest on the chest.

kits.story: Marking Reebok's return to the tournament for the first time since 1998, Panama's home jersey features the traditional red with a repeating harpy eagle pattern woven into the fabric from the national team crest. A polo-style collar with white and blue sleeve trims echoes the national flag, while an embossed federation logo pattern runs throughout.

The away kit features a navy blue base with white side panels and a white polo collar detailed with dark stripes. Gold branding and the national crest complete the design. Panama is the first nation to wear Reebok's Vector logo at the tournament since Paraguay, Chile and Colombia in 1998.

links.title: Reebok announces return with three of the best kits at Football 2026

kits.story: Puma keeps the Albirroja's classic red-and-white stripes but renders the red stripes with a textured, speckled 'dust' effect referencing Paraguay's tierra colorada — the red-earth pitches where generations first played. A royal blue crew-neck collar and matching cuffs complete the flag palette, launched under the campaign 'De la Tierra a la Piel' ('From the Earth to the Skin').

The away shirt is built on a dark navy base with an all-over abstract print in swirling electric blue and teal, a pattern Puma describes as inspired by Paraguay's natural landscape. The fluid graphic covers the front and extends across the sleeves and lower back panel.

links.title: Puma's Paraguay 2026 home jersey — a contemporary twist on traditional stripes

kits.story: Portugal's first shirt under Puma — after the federation's switch from Nike this cycle — is red with green trim on the v-neck collar and sleeves, carrying a subtle tonal wave pattern that references the nation's maritime history, from Henry the Navigator to Vasco da Gama. A compass-inspired detail sits inside the collar, and Puma debuted the kit with a block-party unveiling at Domino Square in Brooklyn.

The away shirt is white with a crashing-wave graphic split into two shades of lagoon green, arranged in a V-shaped silhouette across the chest, shoulders and upper back — a louder expression of the same ocean theme as the home kit. A darker green is used on the collar and side panels, paired with dark-red names and numbers for contrast.

Part of the Puma × Salehe Bembury travel-wear collection released June 4, 2026 across 11 Puma nations; Bembury set each country's pieces on local visual codes, with Portugal's Gray Sky base overlaid by a swirling wave graphic drawn from the country's connection to the Atlantic.

kits.story: The Qatar home jersey stays rooted in the nation's signature maroon, accented by deep white detailing. A centered zigzag chest bar mimics the serrated edge of the Qatari flag, while a subtle tonal pattern inspired by the national flag's nine-pointed serration runs through the fabric. 'Qatar' is written in Arabic on the upper back.

An abstract grey wave-like graphic sweeps across Qatar's away jersey, inspired by the geometry and shapes of the desert dune landscape. 'Qatar' is etched in Arabic across the back of the neck as a symbol of national pride and unity. The adidas Trefoil logo adds a heritage touch to this sand-toned design.

kits.story: Saudi Arabia's home jersey elevates the traditional deep green base with purple and gold accents and a geometric pattern inspired by the ornate doorways that define the nation's architecture. The print is infused with hues lifted from Saudi Arabia's vibrant lavender fields, creating a unique palette. The adidas Trefoil logo returns for this collection.

The away kit features a clean white base with gold and green adidas stripes along the shoulders. The Saudi crest and Trefoil logo are rendered in gold, giving the shirt an elegant, premium feel. The design maintains the architectural motifs from the home kit in a more subtle tonal treatment.

kits.story: The Scotland home kit presents the traditional dark navy base with subtle tonal graphics inspired by the national Saltire, visible across the body and shoulders. A white deep-cut neckline and sleeve cuffs complement the design, while the Scottish flag is placed below the collar on the back. The crest returns to a central position on the chest, produced with adidas Climacool+ and jacquard-knit fabric.

The away kit marks a return to scarlet red, a color not used for Scotland's away strip since the 1980s. Fine vertical purple pinstripes run throughout the body, adding contrast and a sense of movement. The reintroduction of the adidas Trefoil logo connects the design to some of the brand's most celebrated heritage kits. A thistle graphic on the back of the neck nods to Scotland's national flower, symbolizing resilience.

kits.story: Senegal's home kit features a white base covered in a multicolor graphic inspired by the Car Rapide, the iconic hand-painted minibuses of Dakar that serve as moving folk art. The kit hides a remarkable production secret: it is printed inside out, creating muted pastel tones on the exterior while the inside reveals the full-vibrancy pattern featuring lions, baobab trees and traditional graphics in red, yellow and green.

The away kit arrives in the Lions' iconic 'Wild Green' with a striking kaleidoscopic graphic — described by Puma as moving 'like music — rhythm, pride and energy made wearable.' A bold central vertical panel bears geometric and kaleidoscopic shapes inspired by traditional Senegalese textile craftsmanship. A golden star above the FSF crest symbolizes Senegal's continental titles as two-time African champions.
An official warm-up jersey in the national flag's yellow and green, combining Senegalese style with Puma's dryCELL moisture-wicking technology and 100% recycled material.

kits.story: Drawing inspiration from the iconic 2010 jersey, the electric yellow home shirt with green trim honors that legacy while stepping into a new era under adidas. Subtle woven graphics throughout the fabric represent the nation's 12 official languages, a powerful reflection of South Africa's diversity, unity, and shared love for football. Features CLIMACOOL+ technology with 3D body-mapped ventilation.

Built on a Team Dark Green base with tonal vertical striping and intricate geometric detailing, the away jersey features a fold-over collar with white trim and gold adidas Three Stripes on the shoulders. The classic white Trefoil logo returns on the chest alongside the SAFA crest, nodding to the heritage of the 2010 era. SoccerBible shot the campaign bringing together different South African subcultures.

kits.story: adidas' Spain home shirt is red with fine repeating yellow pinstripes drawn from the Spanish flag and the federation crest, offset by navy accents, with 'ESPAÑA' printed at the back of the neck. It belongs to adidas' 1990s-inspired 2026 collection, which returns the Originals Trefoil logo to the tournament for the first time in 36 years.

The away kit sets an intricate pyrite-toned all-over pattern — inspired by the drawings and graphics found in classic books and manuscripts — against an off-white base that evokes the color of a page. Gold and burgundy detail the sleeves and neckline, and 'ESPAÑA' is etched on the back of the neck, its distinctive letter Ñ celebrating the Spanish language and cultural heritage.

adidas' anthem jacket for Spain uses a deep navy base that closely matches the colors of Spain's 2010 away kit, with the oversized Three Stripes across the shoulders shared by adidas' whole 2026 anthem-jacket range. A geometric royal-blue pre-match jersey with red and yellow zigzag stripes was also produced for warm-ups.

kits.story: The adidas Sweden home jersey uses a bright yellow base with royal blue raglan-style shoulder accents, reworked with a retro 1970s edge. A subtle tone-on-tone floral graphic runs through the fabric, inspired by the daisy-pattern stitching found on vintage denim and traditional Swedish folk dresses from the era. 'SVERIGE' is stamped at the back of the collar in 1970s-inspired typography.

Three distinct shades of royal blue combine in a vertically structured layout with repeating geometric wave patterns, drawing visual inspiration from 1970s Scandinavian sportswear and Sweden's music-and-culture scene. A pale yellow V-neckline trim and matching shoulder stripes connect it back to the national colors. The adidas Trefoil logo returns to a Swedish jersey for the first time in decades.

kits.story: Puma's Switzerland 2026 home jersey is built on a deep red base with white accents and an all-over graphic print of swirling, topographic-style lines inspired by the Helvetia page of the Swiss passport, symbolizing Swiss maps, borders and alpine terrain. Under ultraviolet light the chest logos illuminate in neon red, adding a hidden-technology layer to the passport-inspired design.

The away jersey features a pale grey base overlaid with a striking neon-green crystalline pattern evoking topographical maps and the rugged Swiss Alps. Its standout feature is UV-reactive ink: under standard light it shows its subtle palette, but under blacklight the topographic pattern transforms into an electric blue-and-neon-green spectacle — one of the most technically innovative shirts of the 2026 cycle.

kits.story: Kappa's Tunisia home kit features a clean white base paired with bold red accents, celebrating the 'Eagles of Carthage' nickname as its central design theme. An elegant eagle feather graphic sweeps across the shoulders and sleeves, adding sophistication to the slim-fit silhouette crafted from recycled stretch jacquard fabric.

The away jersey inverts the color scheme with a vibrant red base contrasted by sharp white graphics while maintaining the same feather design template as the home kit. Kappa's Kombat Pro 2026/27 technology delivers a performance-focused fit.

kits.story: The home kit restores Türkiye's classic red identity with a Sport Red and Team Red combination. The torso features a flowing ebru-inspired marbling pattern in contrasting dark and bright red tones, referencing Turkey's centuries-old paper marbling art tradition. A bold horizontal chest band houses the Turkish flag centrally with a white Nike Swoosh below.

The away kit builds on a clean white base with a bold Sport Red horizontal band incorporating the same ebru marbling pattern from the home kit. The Turkish flag sits centrally within the red band, while red side panels extend from the underarm to the hem. The design echoes Turkey's historic 1928 look with a contemporary edge.

kits.story: Nike's USMNT home shirt is white with vibrant red wavy stripes across the front and sleeves, designed to mimic a flag rippling in the wind — a direct callback to the kits of 1994, the last time the US hosted the tournament, and to the 2012 'Waldo' shirt. A navy crew-neck collar and cuffs complete the flag palette; Nike involved USMNT players throughout the design process, unveiling the finished kits to the squad in Austin in October 2025.

The away kit is rich navy with subtle tonal stars embellished across the shirt, a nod to the celebrated denim 'stars' jersey of 1994. US Soccer and Nike framed the 2026 pair as 'one stars, one stripes,' both drawn from the American flag itself.

Part of the culture-forward X2 collection from US Soccer, Nike and the Virgil Abloh Archive, the white anthem jacket's thin red and blue striping draws on nautical Americana — specifically the optic-white jackets worn by Dennis Conner's 1987 America's Cup crew — while reimagining graphics from 1994. It released June 15, 2026 via US Soccer, Dover Street Market and SNKRS.

kits.story: A clean execution of Uruguay's iconic Celeste sky blue, paired with navy accents that reinforce clarity and confidence. The design is modelled on several classic Celeste shirts worn through the ages, featuring an immaculate white polo collar and refined touches of navy trim. Imbued with the spirit of garra charrúa — the Uruguayan fighting spirit.

Inspired by the architectural identity of Montevideo, the away kit features an Obsidian base with geometric graphic elements drawn from Art Deco influences found across the city. Specific nods to the Estadio Centenario and the Torre de los Homenajes appear in light-based shapes drawn from the tower's window formations. A bold wings pattern stretches across the chest, symbolizing ascent and ambition.

kits.story: Produced by domestic brand 7Saber for Uzbekistan's historic first-ever tournament appearance, the home kit arrives in the nation's traditional blue. A large, intricate mosaic pattern is sublimated onto the lower torso, paying homage to the country's stunning historical architecture and famous tiled monuments like the Registan. Multi-colored flag striping adorns the crew-neck collar.

The away shirt provides a white alternative with the same architectural mosaic pattern and flag-inspired collar detailing as the home. A distinctive multicolor stripe in teal, green, orange, and white on the collar draws directly from the colors of the Uzbekistan national flag.
